entities.name: Ministry of International Trade and Industry
entities.desc: The Ministry of International Trade and Industry (MITI) was a Japanese government agency responsible for industrial policy, trade promotion, and economic development, playing a key role in Japan's post-war economic growth.

entities.name: United States Small Business Administration
entities.desc: The U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) is a government agency that supports entrepreneurs and small businesses through loans, grants, counseling, and other programs to promote economic growth and job creation.

entities.name: China Unicom
entities.desc: China Unicom is one of the major state-owned telecommunications operators in China, providing mobile, broadband, and other network services nationwide.

entities.name: Policy Coordination Body
entities.desc: A Policy Coordination Body is an organization, typically within the Executive Branch of the U.S. government, that brings together officials from multiple agencies to coordinate, develop, and advise on policy in a specific domain. These bodies are primarily advisory and focus on ensuring coherent and consistent policy implementation across departments. Examples include the National Security Council (NSC), Domestic Policy Council (DPC), and National Economic Council (NEC).

entities.name: Review Board
entities.desc: A Review Board is an organization or committee that evaluates proposals, research, policies, or projects and provides formal recommendations, approvals, or feedback. These boards are typically composed of experts or stakeholders and focus on ensuring quality, compliance, and alignment with organizational objectives. Examples include Institutional Review Boards (IRBs) for research, Ethics Review Boards, and Government Procurement Review Boards.
